title: Holy Sonnet IV: Oh My Black Soul! Now Art Thou Summoned by John Donne
content:
Oh my black soul! now art thou summoned
By sickness, death's herald, and champion;
Thou art like a pilgrim, which abroad hath done
Treason, and durst

title: The Prohibition by John Donne
Holy Sonnet XVI: Father, Part Of His Double Interest by John Donne
content:
Father, part of his double interest
Unto thy kingdom, thy Son gives to me,
His jointure in the knotty Trinity
He keeps, and gives to me his death's co

title: Elegy IX: The Autumnal by John Donne
content:
No spring nor summer Beauty hath such grace
As I have seen in one autumnall face.
Young beauties force our love, and that's a rape,
This doth but coun

title: Holy Sonnet XIX: Oh, To Vex Me, Contraries Meet In One by John Donne
content:
Oh, to vex me, contraries meet in one:
Inconstancy unnaturally hath begot
A constant habit; that when I would not
I change in vows, and in devotion.
A
